item.title: 日本将释放石油储备，美国则呼吁购买美国产品
item.description: 日本计划于周一开始动用战略石油储备，以缓解美以联军对伊朗开战带来的冲击，这令人不禁想起半个世纪前那场促使东京建立战略储备的石油危机。【原文：Japan plans ​to start releasing oil from its stockpiles on Monday to soften the shock from the ‌U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, a stark reminder of the oil crisis half a century ago that prompted Tokyo to create reserves.】

item.title: 据CNBC报道，费蒂塔娱乐公司正就以65亿美元收购凯撒娱乐进行谈判
item.description: 据CNBC周六援引知情人士消息报道，蒂尔曼·费尔蒂塔旗下的费尔蒂塔娱乐公司正就以每股32美元的价格收购凯撒娱乐进行谈判，该交易对凯撒娱乐的估值为65亿美元。【原文：Tilman Fertitta's ​Fertitta Entertainment ‌is negotiating ​to ​buy Caesars Entertainment ⁠for $32 ​per share, ​at an equity value ​of $6.5 ​billion, CNBC reported ‌on ⁠Saturday, citing sources ​close ​to ⁠the situation.】

item.title: 据《The Information》报道，字节跳动因版权纠纷暂停推出视频AI模型
item.description: 据《The Information》周六援引两名直接知情人士的消息报道，在与好莱坞各大电影公司及流媒体平台发生一系列版权纠纷后，字节跳动已暂停其最新视频生成模型Seedance 2.0的全球发布。【原文：ByteDance has put ​on hold ‌the global launch of ​its latest ​video-generation model, Seedance ⁠2.0, ​after a series ​of copyright disputes with major ​Hollywood ​studios and streaming platforms, ‌The ⁠Information reported on Saturday, citing ​two ​people ⁠with direct knowledge ​of the ​situation.】

item.title: 马斯克表示，特斯拉的“巨型”芯片工厂项目将于七天后启动
item.description: 特斯拉首席执行官埃隆·马斯克周六表示，Terafab项目将于七天后启动。【原文：Tesla CEO ​Elon Musk ‌said on Saturday ​that the ​Terafab project will ⁠launch ​in seven ​days.】

item.title: 日本和韩国部长表示，两国已做好应对外汇市场波动的准备
item.description: 日本和韩国周六对本国货币的急速贬值表示担忧，并表示已准备好采取行动应对外汇市场的过度波动。【原文：Japan and South Korea expressed concern on Saturday about the rapid declines in their currencies, saying they ​were ready to act against excessive foreign-exchange volatility.】

item.title: 西屋电气称，美日双方就潜在核电项目中的分工达成一致
item.description: 西屋电气全球业务计划总裁丹·利普曼周六向路透社表示，美国和日本已就双方在涉及西屋电气和日本核电设备制造商的潜在联合核电项目中所扮演的角色达成一致。【原文：The United States and Japan have agreed on the roles they will take in a potential joint nuclear project involving Westinghouse ​and Japanese nuclear power equipment makers, Dan Lipman, president of ‌global business initiatives at Westinghouse, told Reuters on Saturday.】

item.title: 独家：随着人工智能成本不断攀升，Meta计划进行大规模裁员
item.description: 三位知情人士向路透社透露，Meta正计划进行大规模裁员，受影响员工可能占公司总人数的20%或更多。此举旨在抵消其在人工智能基础设施方面的高昂投资，并为人工智能辅助员工带来的更高效率做好准备。【原文：Meta is planning sweeping layoffs that could affect ​20% or more of the company, three sources familiar with the matter told Reuters, as Meta seeks ‌to offset costly artificial intelligence infrastructure bets and prepare for greater efficiency brought about by AI-assisted workers.】

item.title: 精神航空将把机队规模缩减至破产前的三分之一
item.description: 据法庭文件显示，精神航空（Spirit Airlines）的母公司精神航空控股（Spirit Aviation Holdings）周五表示，计划将其机队规模缩减至破产前规模的约三分之一。【原文：Spirit Aviation Holdings , the parent company of Spirit ​Airlines, said on Friday it plans to shrink its fleet to about one‑third of its pre‑bankruptcy size, according ‌to a court filing.】

item.title: 苹果MacBook Neo成为该公司十多年来最易于维修的笔记本电脑
item.description: 据iFixit周五发布的一份分析报告显示，苹果上周发布的MacBook Neo（面向学生的起售价为499美元）是该公司自2014年以来推出的可维修性最高的笔记本电脑。【原文：Apple's MacBook Neo, the laptop it announced last week that starts at $499 for students, is the most repairable laptop ​the company has released since 2014, according to an analysis released Friday by iFixit.】

item.title: Stellantis在美国因“渠道塞货”问题提起的股东诉讼中获判驳回
item.description: 周五，一名美国法官驳回了一起指控汽车制造商Stellantis欺诈股东的诉讼。该诉讼称，Stellantis未披露其通过向经销商倾销过量库存（即所谓的“渠道塞货”）来制造短期销量增长、却导致长期销量下滑的行为。【原文：A U.S. judge on Friday dismissed a ​lawsuit accusing the automaker ‌Stellantis of defrauding shareholders by failing to disclose how it overloaded ​retailers with excessive inventory, ​known as channel stuffing, leading to ⁠short-term sales increases and ​long-term sales declines.】

item.title: Digg因面临AI机器人激增而裁员
item.description: Digg正以当前数字环境中的“残酷现实”以及人工智能驱动的机器人活动激增为由裁员，距离这家曾风靡一时的内容聚合平台宣布重返市场已过去一年多。【原文：Digg is laying off staff citing "brutal reality" in the current digital environment and a surge in artificial intelligence-driven bot ​activity, more than a year after the once-popular content ‌aggregator announced its comeback.】

item.title: 《华尔街日报》报道称，特朗普政府将因促成TikTok交易获得100亿美元佣金
item.description: 据《华尔街日报》周五报道，在近期完成的收购TikTok美国业务的交易中，唐纳德·特朗普总统领导的政府将从投资者那里获得约100亿美元的费用。【原文：President Donald ​Trump's administration ‌is set to ​receive ​a roughly $10 billion ⁠fee ​from investors ​in the recently completed ​deal ​to take control ‌of ⁠TikTok's U.S. business, the ​Wall ​Street ⁠Journal reported ​on Friday.】

item.title: 美国商务部撤回了关于人工智能芯片出口的拟议规则，政府网站显示
item.description: 据政府网站消息，美国商务部周五撤回了其拟议的关于人工智能芯片出口的法规。【原文：The U.S. ‌Department of Commerce on Friday withdrew ​its planned rule on ​AI chip exports, according ⁠to a ​government website.】

item.title: 汽车行业团体敦促特朗普阻止中国汽车进入美国
item.description: 据路透社看到的信函显示，主要汽车行业组织敦促特朗普政府阻止中国汽车制造商进入美国市场，这在特朗普总统计划与中国国家主席习近平会晤之际引发了担忧。【原文：Major auto trade groups urged ​the Trump administration to keep Chinese carmakers out of the U.S., ‌raising concerns ahead of President Donald Trump's planned meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping, according to a letter seen by Reuters.】

item.title: 优步联合创始人卡兰尼克推出Atoms，进军专业机器人领域
item.description: 优步联合创始人兼前首席执行官特拉维斯·卡兰尼克于上周五正式推出其初创公司Atoms，该公司专注于开发专业工业机器人，旨在实现采矿、运输和食品行业的任务自动化。【原文：Travis Kalanick, the co-founder and former chief executive of Uber , on Friday launched his startup, Atoms, focused ​on specialized industrial robotics designed to automate tasks ‌in the mining, transport and food sectors.】

item.title: 据报道，伯克希尔反对关于劳动力监督的股东提案，并披露了巴菲特的薪酬
item.description: 伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司周五表示，其董事会一致敦促否决一项股东提案，该提案要求公司就集团旗下各运营业务在劳动力和人力资本管理方面的监督框架提交一份报告。【原文：Berkshire Hathaway said on Friday its ​board of directors unanimously ‌urged the rejection of a shareholder proposal ​that it produce ​a report on its ⁠oversight framework for workforce ​and human-capital management ​across the conglomerate's operating businesses.】

item.title: 随着物价上涨，美国扩大了对委内瑞拉制裁的豁免范围
item.description: 随着美国对伊朗发动袭击导致价格上涨，美国于周五更新了针对委内瑞拉的制裁豁免条款，旨在为与该国石油及化肥出口相关的投资和活动扫清障碍。【原文：The United States on Friday ​updated waivers to Venezuela sanctions ‌aimed at easing the way for investment and activities related to the ​country's oil and the export ​of fertilizer, as prices rise ⁠amid U.S. strikes on Iran.】

item.title: Adobe同意支付1.5亿美元以解决涉嫌违反《在线购物者保护法》的指控
item.description: 美国司法部周五表示，Adobe公司已同意支付1.5亿美元的和解金并接受禁令，以解决有关该软件公司订阅做法违反《恢复在线购物者信心法》的指控。【原文：The U.S. ​Justice Department ‌said on Friday that ​Adobe ​Inc has agreed ⁠to ​a $150 million ​settlement and an injunction to ​resolve ​allegations that the ‌software ⁠firm's subscription practices violated ​the ​Restore ⁠Online Shoppers' Confidence ​Act.】

item.title: 特朗普下令各机构简化住房建设及抵押贷款的行政审批程序
item.description: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普周五签署了两项行政命令，旨在提高住房可负担性。白宫此举旨在在11月中期选举前解决选民的一项主要关切。【原文：U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday signed two ​executive orders aimed at improving housing affordability as the White House ‌moves to address a key voter concern ahead of November’s midterm elections.】

item.title: 现代汽车因一起致命事故，对部分2026款Palisade SUV下达停售令
item.description: 现代汽车周五表示，由于电动座椅存在问题，且俄亥俄州发生了一起导致一名两岁女童死亡的事故，该公司已发布停售令，并将召回美国和加拿大部分2026款Palisade SUV。【原文：Hyundai Motor said on Friday it is ​issuing a stop sale ‌and will recall some 2026 Palisade SUVs in ​the United States ​and Canada over an issue ⁠with power seats ​following an incident in which ​a two-year-old girl was killed in Ohio.】

item.title: 随着春播临近，伊朗战争导致美国农民无法获得价格合理的化肥
item.description: 美国和加拿大的农民原本就对又将面临低利润甚至亏损的一年感到忧心忡忡，如今他们又因难以购得化肥而可能面临春播受阻的困境。自伊朗战争导致全球贸易陷入瘫痪以来，市场上现有化肥的价格已飙升逾三分之一。【原文：Farmers in the U.S. and Canada, who were already worried about prospects for another year ​of low profits or losses, now could have spring planting disrupted as they struggle to find fertilizer, and prices ‌for any available supplies have spiked more than a third since the war in Iran paralyzed global trade.】

item.title: 欧洲迈出禁止AI生成的儿童性虐待图像的第一步
item.description: 上周五，欧盟各国政府提议将这一条款纳入两年前通过的欧盟里程碑式人工智能法规，欧洲由此迈出了禁止生成儿童性虐待材料的人工智能实践的第一步。【原文：Europe on Friday took the first step towards outlawing artificial ​intelligence practices which generate child sexual ‌abuse material after EU governments proposed to add this provision to the bloc's landmark AI rules adopted ​two years ago.】

item.title: 独家：消息人士称，SpaceX聘请吉博森·邓恩律师事务所和戴维斯·波克·沃德威尔律师事务所作为其重磅IPO的法律顾问
item.description: 据两位知情人士透露，SpaceX及其承销此次重磅IPO的银行已选定两家律师事务所，为该交易提供法律咨询。这是企业在筹备上市时采取的首批具体举措之一。【原文：SpaceX and the banks underwriting its ​blockbuster IPO have chosen two law firms to provide legal advice on the deal, ‌one of the first concrete steps companies take when preparing a public listing, two people familiar with the plans said.】

item.title: 伊朗局势给炼油企业带来压力，加州油价大幅上涨
item.description: 由于若干特殊因素，加州的燃油成本涨幅甚至超过了美国其他地区；预计伊朗战争的连锁反应将使该州的汽油零售价升至每加仑10美元，而航空燃油价格在短短两周内已飙升47%。【原文：Fuel costs in California are soaring even more than in the rest of the U.S. due to several unique factors, with fallout from the war ​in Iran forecast to push pump prices in the state to $10 a gallon and jet fuel prices jumping 47% in just two weeks.】

item.title: 中国开发商富力地产公布了一项总额达47亿美元的全面债务重组计划
item.description: 深陷困境的中国房地产开发商富力地产于周五公布了其离岸债务重组的详细条款，其中包括发行新股、可转换债券及长期有担保票据的计划。【原文：Embattled Chinese property developer Fantasia on Friday unveiled detailed terms of its offshore debt restructuring, including plans to issue ​new equity, convertible bonds and long-dated secured notes.】

item.title: 《金融时报》报道：随着AI编程工作受挫，马斯克再次罢免xAI创始人
item.description: 据《金融时报》周五报道，埃隆·马斯克在其人工智能公司xAI引发了一轮新的裁员潮，由于他对该公司编程部门表现不佳感到不满，又有更多联合创始人被扫地出门。【原文：Elon Musk has triggered a fresh wave of job cuts at his AI firm xAI, with more co-founders pushed out amid his dissatisfaction with ​the underperformance of the startup's coding division, the Financial Times reported on Friday.】

item.title: 亚马逊获胜：卢森堡法院撤销创纪录的8.54亿美元隐私罚款
item.description: 周五，亚马逊针对卢森堡隐私监管机构开出的创纪录7.46亿欧元（8.544亿美元）罚款提起的上诉获得胜诉。法院认定该监管机构未妥善开展分析工作，必须重新评估此案。【原文：Amazon on Friday won its appeal against a record 746-million-euro ($854.4 million) fine imposed by ​Luxembourg’s privacy regulator, after a court found the ‌watchdog had not properly carried out its analysis and must reassess the case.】

item.title: 美国烟草监管机构对电子烟持谨慎态度，但已批准Glas电子烟的许可证
item.description: 根据一份已获许可产品的公开清单，美国食品和药物管理局本周又批准了一个电子烟品牌上市销售，但仅限烟草口味。【原文：The U.S. Food ​and Drug Administration this week granted a licence for sale for one more vape brand, ‌but only in tobacco flavour, according to a public list of licensed products.】

item.title: 五角大楼负责人表示，他热切希望特朗普的盟友收购CNN，同时猛烈抨击该媒体的战争报道
item.description: 五角大楼负责人皮特·赫格塞斯周五表示，他迫切希望特朗普的盟友、派拉蒙-天舞影业首席执行官大卫·埃利森接管美国有线电视新闻网（CNN），同时他批评了美国新闻媒体对伊朗战争的报道。【原文：Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth on Friday said he was ​eager to see Trump ally and Paramount Skydance CEO David Ellison take over CNN, as he criticized ‌the U.S. news media's coverage of the Iran war.】

item.title: 据彭博社报道，波音公司正在维修25架存在布线缺陷的737 MAX客机
item.description: 据彭博社周五援引知情人士的话报道，波音公司正在维修多达25架存在布线缺陷的未交付737 MAX客机，并表示第一季度的交付可能面临延迟。【原文：Boeing ‌is repairing as many as 25 undelivered 737 MAX ​jets with wiring flaws ​and said first-quarter deliveries could ⁠face delays, Bloomberg News ​reported on Friday, citing ​people familiar with the matter.】
